channel.title: Research.google on Chege's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Research.google on Chege's Blog

item.title: Calculators
item.description:
Android Calculator

Making a precise calculator is not trivial. While \(10^{100} + 1 - 10^{100} = 1\), iOS’s calculator gives back \(0\). explores how Android’s calculator gets this right courtesy of .

Real number representations are fundamentally imprecise because we can’t squeeze in infinitely many real numbers into a finite number of bits. Floating-point representations have a base \(\beta\) (assumed to be even) and a precision \(p\), e.g., if \(\beta = 10\) and \(p = 3\), then \(0.1\) is represented as \(1.00 \times 10^{-1}\). IEEE 754, a dominant floating point representation, requires \(\beta = 2, p = 24\) for single precision and \(p = 53\) for double precision. In IEEE 754, \(0.1\) cannot be represented exactly as \(1/10\) is not a factor of \(2\); it is approximately \(1.10011001100110011001101 \times 2^{-4}\).

item.title: Areas of Interest in Computer Science
item.description:
Maybe a good approximation is where Google Research is spending $$$

: Algorithms and Optimization; Augmented Reality and Virtual Reality; Geo; Human Computer Interaction; Health Research; Machine Learning and Data Mining; Mobile; Machine Perception; Networking; Natural Language Processing; Privacy; Quantum Computing; Structured Data, Extraction, Semantic Graph, and Database Management; Security; Software Engineering and Programming Languages; Systems; Misc.

  1. Research Scholar Program recipients. research.google . Apr 7, 2021.
